question.label: Committed to what
question.name: What makes it hard to tell who committed to what in those conversations?
question.description: Drills into the specific source of ambiguity in fast cross-team discussions so the manager can remove it.

answer: Being remote makes it harder to read context that others seem to pick up naturally.

assessment.note: Remote context is making meeting signals and implicit commitments hard to read, which is a strong clarity gap; [THREAD-DEFERRED] 'being remote' is a concrete thread, but drill cap forces stage advance.

new_queue.label: Alignment friction
new_queue.name: What part of the alignment work is taking more effort than it should?

new_queue.label: Manager boundaries
new_queue.name: What boundaries from me would help most right now?

issues: coverage: injected growth into next question (0 touches after turn 4)

history.answer_excerpt: I am settling in, but I still feel like I am learning the unwritten rules in every meeting.
I can draft plans, though I am not always sure when I should decide versus escalate.
I am settling in, but I still feel like I am learning the unwritten rules in every meeting.
Cross-team conversations move fast and I sometimes leave unsure about who committed to what.
Being remote makes it harder to read context that others seem to pick up naturally.
